rationale: The "relaxed bus-jam puzzle" hook is fundamentally mismatched with Helix Jump's fast, reflex-driven bouncing/rotating core — a satisfaction/relaxation pitch undersells the energetic, near-miss tension that actually converts on this title. Visually the muted pastel + grid-parking aesthetic clashes with Helix's neon high-contrast 3D tower, and the puzzle mechanic can't be honestly bridged to a falling-ball arcade loop without bait-and-switch risk (store-listing rejection territory). Only the broad casual audience overlaps; do not greenlight this archetype for Helix Jump — if satisfaction ASMR is the goal, build a Helix-native version (smooth descents, color smash combos) instead.
